page: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.4xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by dedicated AWS Graviton3 processors using the arm64 architecture. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz with the AWS Neoverse-V1 core design. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the hpc7g.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 186.54 GB/sec, and write bandwidth reaches 97.55 GB/sec, both scoring in the strong category. CPU performance is generally average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24,504.24 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13,150.0. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic operations, as shown by its Passmark CPU encryption test score, and is not suited for large-scale LLM inference, yielding a poor 2.3 tokens/sec on 70B models.

The instance provides a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, making it highly efficient for clustered, network-bound workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth offer strong resource density for parallel computing without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is optimized for high-performance computing workloads, including computational fluid dynamics, weather forecasting, molecular dynamics, and memory-intensive scientific simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.4xlarge is a dedicated high-performance computing instance powered by the arm64 AWS Graviton3 processor. Featuring 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs at 2.6 GHz, it delivers 128.0 GB of system memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance, with strong large-block read and write bandwidths, alongside average multi-core CPU capabilities. Conversely, it shows weaker performance in cryptographic operations and large-scale LLM inference. Given its high network throughput and dedicated core allocation, the hpc7g.4xlarge is highly suited for clustered high-performance computing workloads, scientific simulations, and memory-intensive parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.4xlarge is an arm64 HPC instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps networking, optimized for parallel scientific computing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.4xlarge is a dedicated 16-core arm64 HPC instance featuring 128 GB RAM, 200 Gbps networking, and AWS Graviton3 processors.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 high-performance computing instance featuring high memory bandwidth and 200 Gbps networking for parallel scientific workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton3 arm64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Delivers 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for clustered workloads
Achieves top-tier memory bandwidth performance in benchmark testing
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on network-attached storage
Optimized for scientific simulations, molecular dynamics, and parallel computing
